catalog.rationale: The fast member of the family made handheld and telephoto work possible while exposing the material's grain more openly.

reference.relationship: Independent interpretive emulation; researched, not measured

dossier.tagline: Distinct grain, warm color, and compressed reversal latitude at practical speed.
dossier.portrait: KODACHROME 200 is modeled around distinct grain, warm color, and compressed reversal latitude at practical speed. The fast member of the family made handheld and telephoto work possible while exposing the material's grain more openly. Grainlab treats that identity as a linked capture, emulsion, process, and output system—not as a color cast pasted over a digital file.

facts.label: CANON RANK
facts.note: Curatorial significance rank; not a sales chart

chapters.title: Why KODACHROME 200 belongs in the century canon
chapters.lede: The fast member of the family made handheld and telephoto work possible while exposing the material's grain more openly.

details.label: Material
details.value: K-14 color reversal; daylight; nominal speed / EI reference 200.

details.label: Image structure
details.value: cubic crystal interpretation, mixed emulsion mapping, medium presentation-scale grain.

notes: The canon balances cultural use, technical influence, geographic/manufacturer breadth, process diversity, and surviving documentation; rank is curatorial rather than a claimed global sales measurement.

chapters.title: Tone, color, and material signature
chapters.lede: The target is distinct grain, warm color, and compressed reversal latitude at practical speed.

details.label: Curve
details.value: Toe 0.10 · development gamma 1.14 · shoulder 0.18 · scan contrast 1.06.

details.label: Color / spectral posture
details.value: warmRed channel sensitivity and density-dependent crossover; daylight reference balance.

details.label: Grain
details.value: medium correlated dye-cloud density variation; cubic geometry; mixed architecture.

details.label: Halation
details.value: 3% thresholded scene-highlight scatter before the film curve; intentionally stock/process dependent.

notes: Grain is signal-dependent and spatially correlated across the full frame; it is not independent RGB noise or a repeating overlay.

chapters.title: From DSLR or iPhone to a plausible negative
chapters.lede: Begin from a neutral SDR rendering, reduce phone HDR/local tone mapping, and protect both ends: this reversal target has less forgiving toe and shoulder room than a negative.

details.label: Input normalization
details.value: Prefer RAW developed neutrally when available. For HEIC/JPEG, choose the least processed rendition; avoid baked Vivid styles, portrait relighting, clarity, and denoise.

details.label: Dynamic range
details.value: Compress computational HDR gently before emulation, then place exposure precisely; do not ask the reversal curve to rescue clipped source channels.

details.label: White balance
details.value: Neutralize obvious camera casts first, then let the daylight stock balance and emulsion sensitivity create the intended response.

details.label: Hard limit
details.value: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.

notes: Apple ProRAW and camera RAW still need a deliberate neutral development; RAW is not automatically scene-linear or aesthetically neutral after a converter renders it.

chapters.title: A stage-by-stage rendering target
chapters.lede: sRGB decode → -0.03 EV scene placement → 1.045/0.990/0.965 channel sensitivity → toe 0.10, γ 1.14, shoulder 0.18 → K-14 color reversal chemistry → 3% thresholded halation → medium dye-cloud structure

details.label: Scene
details.value: Decode to linear light, apply -0.03 EV and the stock sensitivity before tone shaping.

details.label: Emulsion
details.value: Build the toe, midtone gamma, shoulder, crossover, saturation compression, and correlated image structure as one response.

details.label: Lab / optics
details.value: Model K-14 color reversal, chemical fog, local density contrast, and highlight scatter before the output transform.

details.label: Scan / print
details.value: Preserve the direct positive's black density and limited latitude; scan without flattening it into a negative-like file.

notes: All parameters are bounded artistic approximations derived from published characteristics and process knowledge, not digitized manufacturer sensitometric or spectral measurements.

bestFor: Subjects that benefit from distinct grain, warm color, and compressed reversal latitude at practical speed.
Scenes whose palette and contrast align with the stock rather than fighting it.
watchFor: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.
Stacking a strong camera picture style, computational HDR, or sharpening underneath the emulation.
Treating reversal latitude like a forgiving color negative; clipped channels cannot be recovered.
fieldNotes: Expose for the material idea: distinct grain, warm color, and compressed reversal latitude at practical speed.
Try the profile on neutral daylight, mixed light, skin, foliage, and a flat wall before deciding what its name means.
dossier.disclaimer: KODACHROME 200 is an independent Grainlab artistic emulation informed by cited public literature and historical material. It is not endorsed by Kodak, is not a measured ICC/LUT profile, and cannot reproduce a specific batch, lens, exposure, chemistry, printer, scanner, paper, aging state, or wavelengths absent from the digital source.
